title: 8 Herbal Essential Oils For Hiatus Hernia
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils and teas can help alleviate symptoms of hiatus hernia, a condition where the stomach bulges up into the chest through an opening in the diaphragm. One reason why these herbal remedies are effective is that they can help relax the muscles in the stomach and lower esophageal sphincter, which can become strained or inflamed due to the hernia.  For example, ginger (Zingiber officinale) has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. Fennel (Foeniculum vulgare) has a carminative effect, which means it can help relieve bloating and gas by promoting digestion and reducing discomfort. Licorice root (Glycyrrhiza glabra) has anti-inflammatory properties and can help protect the mucous membranes in the stomach and esophagus from acid damage.  Drinking herbal teas or inhaling essential oils made from these herbs can provide relief from symptoms such as heartburn, acid reflux, and difficulty swallowing. By relaxing the muscles and reducing inflammation, these herbal remedies can help alleviate pressure on the esophagus and allow food to pass through more comfortably.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for hiatus hernia

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains a compound called gingerol, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. Gingerol and shogaol, another key constituent, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract. This can be beneficial for people with hiatus hernia, as inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach can exacerbate symptoms. The warming and spasmolytic properties of gingerol can also help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing for more efficient digestion and reducing the likelihood of stomach acid reflux. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles, Zingiber officinale essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of hiatus hernia.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for hiatus hernia
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Zingiber officinale roots, 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil), and a glass jar with a lid.
Clean and chop the Zingiber officinale roots into small pieces, then fill the glass jar 3/4 full with the chopped roots.
Pour 1 cup of carrier oil over the chopped roots, making sure they are completely covered.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the roots infuse into the oil.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discard the solids, and use the oil as needed for hiatus hernia relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils contains the active constituents anethole and fenchone, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Anethole has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hiatus hernia. Fenchone, on the other hand, has a natural relaxant effect on the muscles in the digestive tract, helping to alleviate symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn. The essential oil's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties also help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing the frequency and severity of hiatus hernia symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation in the digestive tract, Foeniculum vulgare essential oils can help to alleviate the symptoms of hiatus hernia.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal essential oils for hiatus hernia
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Foeniculum vulgare seeds and 1 cup of carrier oil (such as coconut or olive oil).
Heat 1 cup of water in a saucepan and add the Foeniculum vulgare seeds. Steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the seeds. Mix the strained liquid with the carrier oil in a 1:10 ratio.
Transfer the mixture to a dark glass bottle and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the bottle daily.
Strain the mixture again and discard the solids. Your Foeniculum vulgare essential oil is now ready to use for hiatus hernia relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains the bioactive constituents glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the esophagus and stomach, common symptoms of hiatus hernia. Glycyrrhizin also has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help to alleviate heartburn and acid reflux associated with hiatus hernia. The flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach from damage. By reducing inflammation and promoting digestive health, Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils may provide relief from hiatus hernia symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for hiatus hernia
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 4 cups of water. Transfer the roots to a large glass jar.
Combine the roots and water in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 3 hours, then let it cool.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another large glass jar. Discard the roots.
Transfer the liquid to a still or a distillation apparatus. Distill the liquid using steam distillation to separate the essential oil from the water.
Collect the essential oil and store it in a dark glass bottle. Use 2-3 drops of the oil in a diffuser or mix it with a carrier oil for massage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains a compound called menthol, which helps to relax the stomach muscles. Menthol also has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms that can irritate the hiatus hernia. The antispasmodic properties of menthol, combined with its anti-inflammatory effects, can help to soothe the stomach and reduce discomfort associated with hiatus hernia. The essential oil's ability to ease digestive issues can also help to reduce the frequency and severity of hernia symptoms. Additionally, menthol's expectorant properties can help to relieve any associated coughing or respiratory issues that may be exacerbating the condition.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for hiatus hernia
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Mentha x piperita leaves and 1 cup of water.
Combine the leaves and water in a saucepan, heat it over low heat for 10 minutes.
Let the mixture cool, then strain it into a bowl using a cheesecloth.
Transfer the liquid to a clean glass bottle, add 1 tablespoon of glycerin to help preserve the oil.
Leave the bottle in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, then use a dropper to extract the essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils contains a compound called cinnamaldehyde, a potent antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation in the stomach. This reduction in inflammation helps alleviate symptoms of hiatus hernia, such as burning sensations and discomfort. The oil's antispasmodic properties also help relax the muscles in the stomach and esophagus, preventing further irritation and complications. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help prevent acid reflux, a common symptom of hiatus hernia. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation in the stomach muscles, Cinnamomum verum essential oils may provide relief for those experiencing hiatus hernia symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal essential oils for hiatus hernia
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cinnamomum verum leaves and 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water in the saucepan until it starts boiling.
Add the Cinnamomum verum leaves to the boiling water and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the leaves. Let the liquid cool down.
Transfer the cooled liquid to a dark glass bottle and add 1 tablespoon of vegetable glycerin. Shake well.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cuminum cyminum essential oils contains a compound called cuminaldehyde, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. The oil also contains limonene, a compound that has been shown to have a protective effect on the mucous membranes, helping to prevent acid reflux and alleviate symptoms of hiatus hernia. Cuminum cyminum essential oils has been found to have a relaxing effect on the muscles in the esophagus, which helps to prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the throat. The oil's antispasmodic properties also help to calm down the spasms in the digestive tract, which can contribute to the development of hiatus hernia. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the esophagus, Cuminum cyminum essential oils can help to alleviate the symptoms of hiatus hernia and provide relief from discomfort and pain.
remedies.image_alt: Cuminum cyminum herbal essential oils for hiatus hernia
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Cuminum cyminum seeds and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil).
In a small saucepan, heat 1 cup of the carrier oil over low heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of Cuminum cyminum seeds to the heated oil and let it infuse for 2-3 hours.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
Discard the solids and store the Cuminum cyminum essential oil in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains carnosic acid, carnosol, and rosmarinic acid as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach lining. The essential oil's antioxidant properties also help protect the stomach from oxidative stress, which can contribute to hiatus hernia symptoms. Rosmarinic acid, in particular, has been shown to relax the stomach muscles and reduce spasms, which can put pressure on the stomach and cause discomfort. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of hiatus hernia.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for hiatus hernia
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers.
Use a steam distillation apparatus to heat 4 cups of water in a separate container.
Place a strainer or cheesecloth over a heat-resistant container. Add the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers to the strainer.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a clean glass bottle. You should get about 2 tablespoons of essential oil.
Store the essential oil in a cool, dark place and use 5-7 drops in a diffuser or add to a carrier oil for massage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains a high concentration of bioactive constituents, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with hiatus hernia. The anti-inflammatory properties of curcumin can also help alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and acid reflux, common complications of hiatus hernia. Curcumin's ability to relax the muscles in the stomach and esophagus can also help prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus, providing relief from symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of the stomach muscles, Curcuma longa essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of hiatus hernia.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for hiatus hernia
remedy_procedure: Gather 500g of dried Curcuma longa roots and 5 cups of carrier oil (like coconut oil) in a clean glass container.
Combine 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil and 1 teaspoon of lavender oil to the carrier oil for added benefits.
Steam distill or cold press the dried Curcuma longa roots to extract the essential oil. Use a ratio of 1:5 (roots to oil) for 2-3 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container. Discard the solids.
Store the Curcuma longa essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. Use 5-7 drops as needed for relieving hiatus hernia symptoms.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent hiatus hernia in some cases. Certain teas like chamomile and licorice root have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the stomach and reduce pressure on the diaphragm. However, more research is needed to confirm this. Some studies suggest that herbal teas may also improve digestion and reduce symptoms of hiatus hernia, but the exact benefits are still being studied.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can help with hiatus hernia symptoms, but it's essential to choose the right ones. Peppermint and ginger teas can calm the digestive system and reduce discomfort. However, some herbal teas like licorice root tea can worsen the condition. It's best to consume them in moderation, especially if you're new to herbal teas.
faq_timeline: For some people, herbal teas like chamomile, licorice root, and slippery elm may help with hiatus hernia symptoms. It's hard to say exactly how long it takes, but it could be a few days to a week or two before you notice any difference. Everyone's body is different, so results can vary.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for hiatus hernia is often recommended before bed. This is because peppermint, chamomile, and licorice root teas can help relax the stomach muscles and reduce symptoms at night. Having a warm cup before sleep may also ease discomfort and help you get a good night's rest.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for hiatus hernia. This is because some teas contain active ingredients that affect the same bodily systems as your medication. For example, peppermint tea can slow digestion, which may worsen acid reflux symptoms. Ginger tea, on the other hand, can speed up digestion, which may worsen hiatus hernia symptoms.
faq_pregnant: If you have a hiatus hernia and are pregnant, it's best to check the ingredients of herbal teas before drinking them. Some herbal teas can relax the muscles in the stomach, which may make a hiatus hernia worse. Look for teas that are gentle on the stomach and avoid those that could make symptoms worse.
faq_breastfeeding: If you have a hiatus hernia and are breastfeeding, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs might make symptoms worse or interact with medications. For example, peppermint and ginger can relax the muscles, but may worsen acid reflux. Chamomile and licorice root might also affect digestion. It's a good idea to talk to your body and see how it reacts to different teas.
